members.name: _ComputeCost
members.summary: Called when computing the cost between two connected points. Note that this function is hidden in the default [AStarGrid2D] class.

members.name: _EstimateCost
members.summary: Called when estimating the cost between a point and the path's ending point. Note that this function is hidden in the default [AStarGrid2D] class.

members.name: Clear
members.summary: Clears the grid and sets the [member region] to [code]Rect2i(0, 0, 0, 0)[/code].

members.name: FillSolidRegion
members.signature: public void FillSolidRegion(Rect2i region, bool solid)
members.summary: Fills the given [param region] on the grid with the specified value for the solid flag. [b]Note:[/b] Calling [method update] is not needed after the call of this function.

members.name: FillWeightScaleRegion
members.signature: public void FillWeightScaleRegion(Rect2i region, float weightScale)
members.summary: Fills the given [param region] on the grid with the specified value for the weight scale. [b]Note:[/b] Calling [method update] is not needed after the call of this function.

members.name: GetIdPath
members.signature: public Vector2i[] GetIdPath(Vector2i fromId, Vector2i toId, bool allowPartialPath)
members.summary: Returns an array with the IDs of the points that form the path found by AStar2D between the given points. The array is ordered from the starting point to the ending point of the path. If [param from_id] point is disabled, returns an empty array (even if [code]from_id == to_id[/code]). If [param from_id] point is not disabled, there is no valid path to the target, and [param allow_partial_path] is [code]true[/code], returns a path to the point closest to the target that can be reached. [b]Note:[/b] When [param allow_partial_path] is [code]true[/code] and [param to_id] is solid the search may take an unusually long time to finish.

members.name: GetPointDataInRegion
members.summary: Returns an array of dictionaries with point data ([code]id[/code]: [Vector2i], [code]position[/code]: [Vector2], [code]solid[/code]: [bool], [code]weight_scale[/code]: [float]) within a [param region].

members.name: GetPointPath
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array GetPointPath(Vector2i fromId, Vector2i toId, bool allowPartialPath)
members.summary: Returns an array with the points that are in the path found by [AStarGrid2D] between the given points. The array is ordered from the starting point to the ending point of the path. If [param from_id] point is disabled, returns an empty array (even if [code]from_id == to_id[/code]). If [param from_id] point is not disabled, there is no valid path to the target, and [param allow_partial_path] is [code]true[/code], returns a path to the point closest to the target that can be reached. [b]Note:[/b] This method is not thread-safe; it can only be used from a single [Thread] at a given time. Consider using [Mutex] to ensure exclusive access to one thread to avoid race conditions. Additionally, when [param allow_partial_path] is [code]true[/code] and [param to_id] is solid the search may take an unusually long time to finish.

members.name: GetPointPosition
members.summary: Returns the position of the point associated with the given [param id].

members.name: GetPointWeightScale
members.summary: Returns the weight scale of the point associated with the given [param id].

members.name: IsDirty
members.summary: Indicates that the grid parameters were changed and [method update] needs to be called.

members.name: IsInBounds
members.signature: public bool IsInBounds(int x, int y)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [param x] and [param y] is a valid grid coordinate (id), i.e. if it is inside [member region]. Equivalent to [code]region.has_point(Vector2i(x, y))[/code].

members.name: IsInBoundsv
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [param id] vector is a valid grid coordinate, i.e. if it is inside [member region]. Equivalent to [code]region.has_point(id)[/code].

members.name: IsPointSolid
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if a point is disabled for pathfinding. By default, all points are enabled.

members.name: SetPointSolid
members.signature: public void SetPointSolid(Vector2i id, bool solid)
members.summary: Disables or enables the specified point for pathfinding. Useful for making an obstacle. By default, all points are enabled. [b]Note:[/b] Calling [method update] is not needed after the call of this function.

members.name: SetPointWeightScale
members.signature: public void SetPointWeightScale(Vector2i id, float weightScale)
members.summary: Sets the [param weight_scale] for the point with the given [param id]. The [param weight_scale] is multiplied by the result of [method _compute_cost] when determining the overall cost of traveling across a segment from a neighboring point to this point. [b]Note:[/b] Calling [method update] is not needed after the call of this function.

members.name: Update
members.summary: Updates the internal state of the grid according to the parameters to prepare it to search the path. Needs to be called if parameters like [member region], [member cell_size] or [member offset] are changed. [method is_dirty] will return [code]true[/code] if this is the case and this needs to be called. [b]Note:[/b] All point data (solidity and weight scale) will be cleared.

members.name: CellShape
members.summary: The cell shape. Affects how the positions are placed in the grid. If changed, [method update] needs to be called before finding the next path.

members.name: CellSize
members.summary: The size of the point cell which will be applied to calculate the resulting point position returned by [method get_point_path]. If changed, [method update] needs to be called before finding the next path.

members.name: DefaultComputeHeuristic
members.summary: The default [enum Heuristic] which will be used to calculate the cost between two points if [method _compute_cost] was not overridden.

members.name: DefaultEstimateHeuristic
members.summary: The default [enum Heuristic] which will be used to calculate the cost between the point and the end point if [method _estimate_cost] was not overridden.

members.name: DiagonalMode
members.summary: A specific [enum DiagonalMode] mode which will force the path to avoid or accept the specified diagonals.

members.name: JumpingEnabled
members.summary: Enables or disables jumping to skip up the intermediate points and speeds up the searching algorithm. [b]Note:[/b] Currently, toggling it on disables the consideration of weight scaling in pathfinding.

members.name: Offset
members.summary: The offset of the grid which will be applied to calculate the resulting point position returned by [method get_point_path]. If changed, [method update] needs to be called before finding the next path.

members.name: Region
members.summary: The region of grid cells available for pathfinding. If changed, [method update] needs to be called before finding the next path.

members.name: Size
members.summary: The size of the grid (number of cells of size [member cell_size] on each axis). If changed, [method update] needs to be called before finding the next path.

values.name: CELL_SHAPE_ISOMETRIC_DOWN
values.summary: Diamond cell shape (for isometric look). Cell coordinates layout where the horizontal axis goes down-right, and the vertical one goes down-left.

values.name: CELL_SHAPE_ISOMETRIC_RIGHT
values.summary: Diamond cell shape (for isometric look). Cell coordinates layout where the horizontal axis goes up-right, and the vertical one goes down-right.

values.name: CELL_SHAPE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum CellShape] enum.

values.name: DIAGONAL_MODE_ALWAYS
values.summary: The pathfinding algorithm will ignore solid neighbors around the target cell and allow passing using diagonals.

values.name: DIAGONAL_MODE_AT_LEAST_ONE_WALKABLE
values.summary: The pathfinding algorithm will avoid using diagonals if at least two obstacles have been placed around the neighboring cells of the specific path segment.

values.name: DIAGONAL_MODE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum DiagonalMode] enum.

values.name: DIAGONAL_MODE_NEVER
values.summary: The pathfinding algorithm will ignore all diagonals and the way will be always orthogonal.

values.name: DIAGONAL_MODE_ONLY_IF_NO_OBSTACLES
values.summary: The pathfinding algorithm will avoid using diagonals if any obstacle has been placed around the neighboring cells of the specific path segment.

values.name: HEURISTIC_CHEBYSHEV
values.summary: The [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chebyshev_distance]Chebyshev heuristic[/url] to be used for the pathfinding using the following formula: [codeblock] dx = abs(to_id.x - from_id.x) dy = abs(to_id.y - from_id.y) result = max(dx, dy) [/codeblock]

values.name: HEURISTIC_EUCLIDEAN
values.summary: The [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_distance]Euclidean heuristic[/url] to be used for the pathfinding using the following formula: [codeblock] dx = abs(to_id.x - from_id.x) dy = abs(to_id.y - from_id.y) result = sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] This is also the internal heuristic used in [AStar3D] and [AStar2D] by default (with the inclusion of possible z-axis coordinate).

values.name: HEURISTIC_MANHATTAN
values.summary: The [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taxicab_geometry]Manhattan heuristic[/url] to be used for the pathfinding using the following formula: [codeblock] dx = abs(to_id.x - from_id.x) dy = abs(to_id.y - from_id.y) result = dx + dy [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] This heuristic is intended to be used with 4-side orthogonal movements, provided by setting the [member diagonal_mode] to [constant DIAGONAL_MODE_NEVER].

values.name: HEURISTIC_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Heuristic] enum.

values.name: HEURISTIC_OCTILE
values.summary: The Octile heuristic to be used for the pathfinding using the following formula: [codeblock] dx = abs(to_id.x - from_id.x) dy = abs(to_id.y - from_id.y) f = sqrt(2) - 1 result = (dx < dy) ? f * dx + dy : f * dy + dx; [/codeblock]

constants.name: HEURISTIC_EUCLIDEAN
constants.summary: The [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_distance]Euclidean heuristic[/url] to be used for the pathfinding using the following formula: [codeblock] dx = abs(to_id.x - from_id.x) dy = abs(to_id.y - from_id.y) result = sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] This is also the internal heuristic used in [AStar3D] and [AStar2D] by default (with the inclusion of possible z-axis coordinate).

constants.name: HEURISTIC_MANHATTAN
constants.summary: The [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taxicab_geometry]Manhattan heuristic[/url] to be used for the pathfinding using the following formula: [codeblock] dx = abs(to_id.x - from_id.x) dy = abs(to_id.y - from_id.y) result = dx + dy [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] This heuristic is intended to be used with 4-side orthogonal movements, provided by setting the [member diagonal_mode] to [constant DIAGONAL_MODE_NEVER].

constants.name: HEURISTIC_OCTILE
constants.summary: The Octile heuristic to be used for the pathfinding using the following formula: [codeblock] dx = abs(to_id.x - from_id.x) dy = abs(to_id.y - from_id.y) f = sqrt(2) - 1 result = (dx < dy) ? f * dx + dy : f * dy + dx; [/codeblock]

constants.name: HEURISTIC_CHEBYSHEV
constants.summary: The [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chebyshev_distance]Chebyshev heuristic[/url] to be used for the pathfinding using the following formula: [codeblock] dx = abs(to_id.x - from_id.x) dy = abs(to_id.y - from_id.y) result = max(dx, dy) [/codeblock]

constants.name: HEURISTIC_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Heuristic] enum.

constants.name: DIAGONAL_MODE_ALWAYS
constants.summary: The pathfinding algorithm will ignore solid neighbors around the target cell and allow passing using diagonals.

constants.name: DIAGONAL_MODE_NEVER
constants.summary: The pathfinding algorithm will ignore all diagonals and the way will be always orthogonal.

constants.name: DIAGONAL_MODE_AT_LEAST_ONE_WALKABLE
constants.summary: The pathfinding algorithm will avoid using diagonals if at least two obstacles have been placed around the neighboring cells of the specific path segment.

constants.name: DIAGONAL_MODE_ONLY_IF_NO_OBSTACLES
constants.summary: The pathfinding algorithm will avoid using diagonals if any obstacle has been placed around the neighboring cells of the specific path segment.

constants.name: DIAGONAL_MODE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum DiagonalMode] enum.

constants.name: CELL_SHAPE_ISOMETRIC_RIGHT
constants.summary: Diamond cell shape (for isometric look). Cell coordinates layout where the horizontal axis goes up-right, and the vertical one goes down-right.

constants.name: CELL_SHAPE_ISOMETRIC_DOWN
constants.summary: Diamond cell shape (for isometric look). Cell coordinates layout where the horizontal axis goes down-right, and the vertical one goes down-left.

constants.name: CELL_SHAPE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum CellShape] enum.

virtualMethods.name: _ComputeCost
virtualMethods.summary: Called when computing the cost between two connected points. Note that this function is hidden in the default [AStarGrid2D] class.

virtualMethods.name: _EstimateCost
virtualMethods.summary: Called when estimating the cost between a point and the path's ending point. Note that this function is hidden in the default [AStarGrid2D] class.
